programs.title: Khush Naseebi
programs.desc:
Khush Naseebi is a heartfelt story of Siraj Qasmi and his three daughters, Narmeen, Maheen, and Emaan.
Siraj Qasmi is a widower who loves his daughters deeply. Narmeen and Maheen are the light of his life, but his heart remains distant from his youngest, Emaan. He carries a silent belief that her birth took away his wife, and that shadow has never left their relationship.
In the same neighborhood lives Humayun, a hardworking tailor and Siraj’s favorite. He quietly loves Narmeen, but keeps his feelings hidden, believing he is not worthy of her.
Like every father, Siraj dreams of seeing his daughters happily married. But before he can fulfill this wish, destiny knocks on his door.
What does fate have in store for Siraj Qasmi? Will Humayun finally confess his love to Narmeen? And will Narmeen ever realize how deeply he loves her?
Geo Entertainment Presentation

programs.title: Shaidai
programs.desc:
Shaidai is a celebration of love, a story that dares to ask what happens when the most beautiful feeling in the world turns into its greatest test.
Ali Khan stands at the peak of the world others only dream of. Driven, relentless, and accustomed to getting what he wants, yet beneath that iron exterior lives a quietly devoted grandson.
Miral is a self made girl who wants to do everything on her own. Vivid, sharp tongued, and fiercely certain of her own path, she moves through life with a conviction that refuses to be dimmed.
Fate, indifferent to logic and timing, brings their names together. But love, it seems, has made enemies before they even knew it existed. From the shadows, forces driven by old grievances and hidden motives begin to close in, testing not just their bond but everything they believe in.
What storms lie ahead in Ali and Miral’s love? Can two people who found each other against all odds hold on when the odds turn against them? And when love is pushed to its breaking point, who will be left standing?
7th Sky Entertainment Presentation

programs.title: Hasna Mana Hai
programs.desc:
There is a locality in the old city of Lahore where a young man (Host: Tabish Hashmi) has come to reside from Karachi. He is the guy who has reside in Canada too, so there is a double introduction with his name. This locality is basically a pulse of society in that almost all the segments are presents. The young man (Host) tries to understand the eccentric behavior of the people around and in doing so inadvertently offers himself to mock at. The host brings to light the fact that the morals are customs more preached than practiced while traditions are customs more practiced than preached. Gradually the host will lay bare the sensibility, psyche and sensitivity of different characters thus giving the viewers insight into the show storyline. The viewers come to know of perplexing paradoxes in the funniest way possible.
In every new episode the locality is entrapped into a tricky situation which they are struggling to come out of employing street smart tactics. Notwithstanding with the fact that they condemn every other person, institution, norm and policy of the government but they exactly do contrarily to what they profess.
The issues of the community revolve around small scale corruption, partisanship, political misadventures, jealousy, interference, snobbery, belittling others. They keep taunting one another using tongue in cheek remarks thus manifesting a past perennially plagued with street power struggle, political affiliation, and proclivity for easy money and predisposition to the influence.
There are two different strands lurking parallel to each other in the show. The host has to entertain the audience by introducing them to socio-political anecdotes, guests, short skits, weird people, achievers, trivial but tantalizing smaller wins, social eccentricity, literary uniqueness, cultural exclusivity, musical performance, parodying, stand up comedy etc and still the other strand runs on the floor as the main subject of the show - the drama being played out by the permanent characters of the show. These permanent characters are Pehalwan, Policeman, Professor, Pehalwan's daughter, a wannabe actress, a foreign returned Pakistani, Pehalwan's wife, a shop keeper, a poet, a fatman (an idle youth), a girl singer.
The show will be divided into different segments but this segmentation is not conventional. The segments will only be announced in terms of the introduction of characters or performance if need be. Usually the characters will come on their own giving an impression as if they are unwanted though the host is quite familiar with them and poses as if their forced entry is not highly welcomed.

programs.title: Case No.9
programs.desc:
Case No 9 is an inspiring story of Sehar, a courageous and ambitious young woman who challenges societal norms and becomes a beacon of resistance for others.
Sehar, confident and driven, joins the company of Kamran, a domineering businessman feared by everyone for his egoistic and controlling nature. Unlike others, Sehar refuses to conform, letting her talent and integrity shine.
Sehar’s defiance unsettles Kamran, who sets out to teach her a lesson. What follows is Sehar’s journey of redemption and resilience as she navigates the challenges thrown her way.
Will Kamran succeed in teaching Sehar a lesson, or will she rise above his schemes? Can Sehar find the strength and support to overcome the odds, or will Kamran’s wealth and influence prove insurmountable?
Geo and 7th Sky Entertainment Presentation

programs.title: Humrahi
programs.desc:
What begins as a series of light-hearted yet unpleasant encounters between Sayhaan and Elif eventually blossoms into an unexpected and deep-rooted love. Just as they begin to find their footing together, the life of Sayhaan Ghazi is suddenly upended by a storm he did not create. When an unforeseen crisis strikes, the two pillars of his existence—his devoted love, Elif, and his formidable father—are forced to face an impossible ultimatum.
Driven by a desperate, protective instinct, they make a difficult choice to shield him from a reality they believe would be his undoing. However, this well-intentioned sacrifice triggers a catastrophic chain of events, turning their home into a place of chilling distance and lifelong regret. It is a haunting exploration of how the choices made for those we love can ultimately become the very walls that permanently divide a family.
Will Sayhaan’s journey forward be defined by the choices others made, or will he find a way to reclaim his own destiny? What does life look like for Elif and Sayhaan’s father when the person they tried to protect can no longer look them in the eye?
Director &
